channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Boy faces arson charge over attack on northwest London synagogue
item.description: A young boy has been charged with arson following an incident at a synagogue in northwest London. The attack, which took place earlier this week, has sparked outrage in the local community. Authoritie...

item.title: How to Observe the Lyrids Meteor Shower This April
item.description: The Lyrids meteor shower, a classic celestial event, will reach its peak from the night of April 21 to the early morning of April 22, 2026. Visible primarily from the northern hemisphere, this spectac...

item.title: The complex role of trophy hunting in African wildlife conservation
item.description: The article explores the controversial practice of trophy hunting in Africa, focusing on whether killing a limited number of wild animals can contribute to conservation. Set in Mozambique’s vast Niass...

item.title: UK Unemployment Drops Unexpectedly, Driven by Student Inactivity
item.description: The UK’s unemployment rate has taken an unexpected drop, falling to 4.9% in the three months to February, according to the Office for National Statistics (ONS). This contrasts with previous prediction...

item.title: US Officials, Including VP Vance, Head to Pakistan for Potential Iran Peace Talks Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance, along with senior US officials, is scheduled to travel to Pakistan for a potential second round of negotiations with Iran regarding the ongoing conflict. The discussions, p...

item.title: PMDC refers cases of medical student suicides and OT video leak to disciplinary committee
item.description: The Pakistan Medical and Dental Council (PMDC) held preliminary hearings on Monday for three serious cases related to medical institutions and referred them to the Disciplinary Committee for final dec...

item.title: Fuel prices cut again as diesel, gasoline, and kerosene drop for second consecutive week
item.description: Fuel prices in the Philippines are set to decline again on Tuesday, April 21, marking the second straight week of rollbacks after a prolonged period of increases linked to geopolitical tensions in the...

item.title: Lagos Free Zone and CEVA Logistics partner to improve logistics in West Africa
item.description: Lagos Free Zone (LFZ) and CEVA Logistics have announced a joint venture to enhance logistics solutions in West Africa. This strategic partnership aims to create a seamless logistics hub within the Lek...

item.title: Iran’s Hardline Military Ascends Amid Power Vacuum Following Assassinations
item.description: Following the targeted killings of senior Iranian leaders by the US and Israel, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has solidified its influence within Iran's political structure. With the de...

item.title: Counterfactual view on Iran’s historical role and Gulf politics
item.description: In this thoughtful opinion piece, Jawed Naqvi invites readers to assume Iran never existed, following the style of Perry Mason’s ‘assuming and not conceding’ approach. The writer explores how this abs...

item.title: JV Ejercito calls for Bato dela Rosa to waive salary due to prolonged absence from Senate
item.description: Senate Deputy Majority Leader and Ethics Committee Chair, Senator JV Ejercito, has called on Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa to voluntarily forgo his salary and allowances amid his absence from Senate...

item.title: California Lawmakers Consider Regulations on Companion Chatbots After Teen Suicide Case
item.description: A California mother, Maria Raine, is advocating for stricter regulations on companion chatbots after the death of her teenage son, Adam, who died by suicide in April 2025. Raine claims her son had sha...

item.title: Blue Origin's New Glenn Mission Experiences Second Stage Anomaly
item.description: Blue Origin's latest New Glenn rocket mission, the third flight of the heavy-lift launcher in its 7x2 configuration, has encountered a significant setback related to the performance of its second stag...

item.title: Rescue of large bird of prey at Welsh caravan site on 11 April 2026
item.description: On 11 April 2026, a dramatic rescue unfolded at a caravan site in Wales involving a large bird of prey. The incident, captured in a BBC News video, highlights the efforts of local wildlife rescuers re...

item.title: Jesy Nelson's car stolen, including vital hospital kit for children
item.description: Jesy Nelson, previously a member of the popular girl group Little Mix, has experienced a distressing incident where her car was stolen while it contained essential hospital supplies for children. The ...

item.title: Victor Wembanyama becomes youngest unanimous NBA Defensive Player of the Year
item.description: Victor Wembanyama, di San Antonio Spurs big man wey stand 7ft 4in tall, don make history for NBA as e clinch di Defensive Player of di Year award with unanimous vote. Di French star collect all di 100...

item.title: Uncertainty surrounds US-Iran talks amid rising tensions and mixed signals
item.description: The editorial discusses the uncertain situation surrounding the proposed US-Iran peace talks expected to take place in Islamabad, noting that the negotiations are hanging in the balance due to rising ...

item.title: Gift Orban Apologizes for Physical Altercation with Fan After Verona Match
item.description: Hellas Verona striker, Gift Orban, has issued a heartfelt apology following a physical incident with a fan after their recent 1-0 defeat to AC Milan. The drama unfolded outside the Stadio Bentegodi, w...

item.title: Pakistan extends restriction on Indian aircraft access to its airspace amid ongoing tensions
item.description: Pakistan Airport Authority (PAA) has extended its restriction on Indian aircraft using Pakistani airspace until May 24, 2026, continuing a policy that has now remained in place for nearly a year. Acco...

item.title: Long queues strain Spanish registry offices after migrant regularisation programme begins
item.description: Spain has experienced significant pressure on its public administration services following the launch of a large-scale migrant regularisation programme approved by the government. The initiative, whic...
